final_prompt.prompt:
Create an original, non-infringing editorial photo of Varanasi's Ganges River morning ritual, capturing the spiritual atmosphere of Indian culture.

Style/medium: documentary street photography, National Geographic editorial style, photorealistic humanist photography in the spirit of Steve McCurry
Composition: wide-angle establishing shot at dawn, 35mm lens, layered depth with foreground devotees and background boats, eye-level perspective
Subject: Hindu devotees performing morning ritual bath, traditional dhotis and saris, hands folded in prayer, serene expressions, partially immersed in water
Scene/backdrop: Ganges River at Varanasi, wooden boats floating through morning mist, ancient ghats with stone steps, distant temple silhouettes
Lighting/mood: golden hour sunrise, warm directional light cutting through mist, soft diffused glow on water surface, reverent and timeless atmosphere
Color palette: warm amber and gold as dominant tones, muted blue-grey mist as secondary, saffron and maroon from traditional clothing as accents
Materials/textures: weathered wooden boat hulls, rippling water with golden reflections, rough stone ghat steps, wet fabric clinging to skin
Avoid: oversaturated colors, tourist snapshot aesthetic, lens flare abuse, 8K stuffer labels, artificial HDR look, cultural stereotypes
